title: Erigeron hispidus
taxonomy: Erigeron hispidus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is commonly known as the hairy fleabane. This plant is native to eastern North America. The classification of Erigeron hispidus includes the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, genus Erigeron, and species E. hispidus. Erigeron hispidus is closely related to other species within the Erigeron genus.
uses: Erigeron hispidus, also known as Daisy Fleabane, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's roots and leaves are said to possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. Erigeron hispidus is also believed to have expectorant properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Additionally, the plant's antiseptic properties make it a potential treatment for gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and ulcers. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Erigeron hispidus for medicinal purposes.

intro: Erigeron hispidus, commonly known as rough fleabane, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
